summary: Well-structured product-ideal blueprint with strong self-description and causal clarity. Policy.*.means() blocks carry dense operational meaning and the when/then chains clearly express the recovery topology (access attempt → repair → escalation → idle teardown). Main weakness is abstraction-level bleed: several policies reference implementation-resolved details (AWS runtime configuration, EC2 user data, Cloudflare origin cert, systemd, specific tunnel runtime tokens) that belong in an implementation-resolved companion rather than a product ideal. The file also contains a rejected Agentish element (line-comment "// Dashboard Recovery"). The when() chains at the bottom repeat policy requirements already established earlier, slightly reducing semantic density. Obsolescence risk is moderate because the infrastructure-specific details (Cloudflare tunnels, Lambda, AWS) could stale independently of the product-level intent.
strengths: Excellent local recoverability: a reader can fully understand the recovery system's intent, failure taxonomy, and lifecycle policy from this file alone without external context
Strong causal clarity: the when/then chains cleanly express the trigger→repair→escalate→idle-teardown topology with explicit failure classification between origin and tunnel failures
Policy.*.means() blocks are high-quality semantic expansions that preload agent reasoning about rationale, failure modes, and tradeoffs
Good entity naming throughout — Manager.dashboardControl, Policy.classifyFailuresSeparately, etc. carry meaning without needing lookup
The enforces() block concisely captures the non-negotiable product constraints
weaknesses: Abstraction discipline bleeds into implementation-resolved territory: references to AWS runtime configuration vs EC2 user data, Cloudflare origin cert vs tunnel runtime token, and systemd belong in a lower-layer companion file
Contains a line comment ('// Dashboard Recovery') which Agentish explicitly rejects

When/then chains at the bottom repeat policy requirements (e.g. classifyFailuresSeparately, preferNamedTunnelWhenConfigured, tunnelCooldown) already bound in earlier chains, reducing semantic density without adding causal information
No live-use evidence recoverable from the file content itself — no references to implementation files, tests, or state artifacts that would confirm active pairing
